q: The ratio of spin-only magnetic moment values  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi>eff</mi></msub><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cr</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>CN</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi>eff</mi></msub><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cr</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> is _______
question: The ratio of spin-only magnetic moment values  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi>eff</mi></msub><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cr</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>CN</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>/</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi>eff</mi></msub><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Cr</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> is _______
solution:
In case of Cr3+ ion , the valence shell electronic configuration is same whether the ligand is strong field or weak field.

The spin only magnetic moment , μeff=nn+2BM

The valence shell electronic configuration of Cr3+ is 3d3, hence, it has three unpaired electrons.

μeff  of Cr(CN)6-3=15 B.M. 

μeff of CrH2O6+3=15 B.M. 

Ratio = 1

q: The major product B in the following sequence of reactions is:
question: The major product B in the following sequence of reactions is:
solution: Alcohol upon reaction with protic acids tends to lose a molecule of water to form alkenes. These reactions are known as dehydrogenation or dehydration of alcohols. It is an example of an elimination reaction.The order of the ease of dehydration of alcohols is: tertiary > secondary > primary.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): A solution of NiH2O62+ is green in colour.
Statement (II): A solution of Ni(CN)42- is colourless.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement (I): A solution of NiH2O62+ is green in colour.
Statement (II): A solution of Ni(CN)42- is colourless.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
solution:
In NiH2O62+, H2O is a weak field ligand. Therefore, there are unpaired electrons in Ni2+. In this complex, the d electrons from the lower energy level can be excited to the higher energy level i.e., the possibility of d-dtransition is present. Hence, NiH2O62+ is coloured.

In NiCN42-, the electrons are all paired as CN- is a strong field ligand. Therefore, d-d transition is not possible in NiCN42-. Hence, it is colourless.

NiH2O6+2→Green colour solution due to d-d transition.

Ni(CN)4-2→is diamagnetic and it is colourless.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right]_6\right]^{3+}, \mathrm{Co}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{NH}_3$ is S.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
In $\mathrm{SF}_6$, Hybridisation state of sulphur is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
In $\left[\mathrm{CrF}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Cr}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^3$
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
$\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Co}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 \mathrm{~F}^{-}$is W.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Co}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
$\left[\mathrm{Mn}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 \mathrm{CN}^{-}$is S.F.L Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Mn}^{3+}$ is $\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$
$\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_6\right]^{3-}, \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}:[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$is W.F.L
Hybridisation state of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$is $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
Total number of $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$ hybridized molecules is 3

subject: Chemistry
q: The molecule/ion with square pyramidal shape is:
question: The molecule/ion with square pyramidal shape is:
solution:
BrF5 is an interhalogen compound, meaning that the molecules of this compound contain two or more different halogen atoms but no atoms of the elements belonging to any other group.

The hybridisation of bromine pentafluoride forms an sp3d2 hybridisation, resulting in a square pyramidal molecular structure. The hybrid molecule has five sigma bonds due to the covalent electron sharing.

subject: Chemistry
q: Consider the metal complexes $[Ni(en)_3]^{2+}$ (A), $[NiCl_4]^{2-}$ (B) and $[Ni(NH_3)_6]^{2+}$ (C). Choose the CORRECT option by considering the number of unpaired electrons present in (A), (B) and (C) respectively and the order of frequency of absorption.
question: Consider the metal complexes $[Ni(en)_3]^{2+}$ (A), $[NiCl_4]^{2-}$ (B) and $[Ni(NH_3)_6]^{2+}$ (C). Choose the CORRECT option by considering the number of unpaired electrons present in (A), (B) and (C) respectively and the order of frequency of absorption.
solution:
The oxidation state of $Ni$ in all three complexes is $+2$. The electronic configuration of $Ni^{2+}$ is $[Ar] 3d^{8}$.

In $[Ni(en)_3]^{2+}$ (A), the coordination number is $6$ (octahedral geometry). The $d^{8}$ configuration in an octahedral field is $t_{2g}^{6} e_{g}^{2}$, which has $2$ unpaired electrons.

In $[NiCl_4]^{2-}$ (B), the coordination number is $4$ with weak field $Cl^{-}$ ligands (tetrahedral geometry). The $d^{8}$ configuration in a tetrahedral field is $e^{4} t_{2}^{4}$, which has $2$ unpaired electrons.

In $[Ni(NH_3)_6]^{2+}$ (C), the coordination number is $6$ (octahedral geometry). The $d^{8}$ configuration in an octahedral field is $t_{2g}^{6} e_{g}^{2}$, which has $2$ unpaired electrons.

Thus, the number of unpaired electrons in (A), (B), and (C) are $2, 2,$ and $2$ respectively.

The frequency of absorption of light is directly proportional to the crystal field splitting energy ($\Delta$).

According to the spectrochemical series, the field strength of the ligands follows the order: $en > NH_3 > Cl^{-}$.

Furthermore, the crystal field splitting energy for octahedral complexes is significantly greater than that for tetrahedral complexes ($\Delta_o = \dfrac{9}{4}\Delta_t$).

Therefore, the order of crystal field splitting energy, and hence the frequency of absorption, is $[Ni(en)_3]^{2+} > [Ni(NH_3)_6]^{2+} > [NiCl_4]^{2-}$, which corresponds to $(A) > (C) > (B)$.

Answer: $2, 2, 2$ and $(A)>(C)>(B)$

q: The spin only magnetic moment of the complex present in Fehling's reagent is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math> (Round off your answer to the nearest integer)
question: The spin only magnetic moment of the complex present in Fehling's reagent is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>.</mo></math> (Round off your answer to the nearest integer)
solution:
Fehling's solution consists of two parts A and B.  Fehling’s solution A  is deep blue colour due to the bis(tartrate) complex of Cu2+. Fehling's solution B is the Rochelle salt (potassium sodium tartrate tetrahydrate) serves as a chelating agent in the solution.

Fehling solution is a complex of Cu++

Cu++=3 d9

Number of unpaired e-=1

Magnetic Moment=11+2=3=1.73BM

q: Consider that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></math> metal ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> forms a complex with aqua ligands, and the spin only magnetic moment of the complex is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.90</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi>BM</mi></math>. The geometry and the crystal field stabilization energy of the complex is :
question: Consider that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></math> metal ion <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></mfenced></math> forms a complex with aqua ligands, and the spin only magnetic moment of the complex is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.90</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi>BM</mi></math>. The geometry and the crystal field stabilization energy of the complex is :
solution:
Since spin only magnetic moment is 4.90 BM so number of unpaired electrons must be 4. There are two possibilities:

(1) If the complex is octahedral, then it has to be high spin complex with configuration t2g2,1,1eg1,1, in that case CFSE=4×-0.4Δ0+2×0.6Δ0=-0.4Δ0
(2) If the complex is tetrahedral then its electronic configuration will be =egt2g2,11,1,1 and CFSE will be=3X -0.6Δt+3X0.4Δt=-0.6Δt

q:
The Spin only magnetic moment value of square planar complex PtNH32ClNH2CH3Cl is _______ B.M. (Nearest integer)
( Given atomic number for Pt=78)
question:
The Spin only magnetic moment value of square planar complex PtNH32ClNH2CH3Cl is _______ B.M. (Nearest integer)
( Given atomic number for Pt=78)
solution:
Spin only magnetic moment can be calculated by the following formula
Magnetic moment =n(n+2)
Where, n is number of unpaired electrons.

In the given compound, Pt2+d8

Pt2+→dsp2 hybridization and have no unpaired electrons..

∴ Magnetic moment

 =n(n+2)=0(0+2)=0

q: When concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> is added to aqueous solution of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>, its colour changes from reddish pink to deep blue. Which complex ion gives blue colour in reaction?
question: When concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> is added to aqueous solution of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>, its colour changes from reddish pink to deep blue. Which complex ion gives blue colour in reaction?
solution:
On addition of conc. HCl. The equilibrium between two coloured Co (II) species can be described by equation

CoH2O6aq2++4Claq-⇌CoCl4aq2-+6H2OℓPinkBlue

The equilibrium is disturbed by changing the conc. of Cl- ions by addition of conc. HCl in forward direction.

q: The values of the crystal field stabilization energies for a high spin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>d</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></math> metal ion in octahedral and tetrahedral fields, respectively, are:
question: The values of the crystal field stabilization energies for a high spin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>d</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></math> metal ion in octahedral and tetrahedral fields, respectively, are:
solution:
For d6 configuration, high spin

(i) In case of octahedral complex tgg2.1.1,eg1.1

CFSE=-0.4nt2g+0.6negΔ0+n(P)

=[-0.4×4+0.6×2]Δ0+0

=-0.4Δ0

(i) In case of tetrahedral complex e2,1,t21,1,1

CFSE=-0.6ne+0.4nt2Δr

=[-0.6×3+0.4×3]Δt

=-0.6Δt

solution:
${ }_{27} \mathrm{Co}^{2+}$-$\mathrm{1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^7 4 s^0}$
As $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$is weak field ligand so no pairing up. Hence it is $sp^3$ hybridized giving tetrahedral geometry. $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$-$\mathrm{1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^6 4 s^0}$
Due to $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$, back pairing is not observed so it will be $\mathrm{sp}^3$ hybridized giving tetrahedral geometry. $\mathrm{Ni}^{2+}-1 \mathrm{s}^2 2 \mathrm{s}^2 2 \mathrm{p}^6 3 \mathrm{s}^2 3 p^6 3 \mathrm{d}^8 4 \mathrm{s}^0$
Because weak ligand, back pairing is not observed so it will be $\mathrm{sp}^3$ i.e. tetrahedral geometry. All the complexes of $\mathrm{Pt}^{2+}$ are square planar including those with weak field ligand such as halide ions thus (D) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Identify the CORRECT set of details from the following:
A. $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}$ : Inner orbital complex; $\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
B. $\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3} \mathrm{~d}^{2}$ hybridized
C. $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
D. $\left[\mathrm{FeF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3} \mathrm{~d}^{2}$ hybridized
E. $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}$ : Inner orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Identify the CORRECT set of details from the following:
A. $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right]^{3+}$ : Inner orbital complex; $\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
B. $\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3} \mathrm{~d}^{2}$ hybridized
C. $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
D. $\left[\mathrm{FeF}_{6}\right]^{3-}$ : Outer orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3} \mathrm{~d}^{2}$ hybridized
E. $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_{4}\right]^{2-}$ : Inner orbital complex; $\mathrm{sp}^{3}$ hybridized
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Analyzing each complex based on Valence Bond Theory (VBT):
A. $[Co(NH_3)_6]^{3+}$:
$Co^{3+}$ is $3d^6$. $NH_3$ is a strong field ligand (SFL) for $Co^{3+}$, causing pairing. This leaves two $3d$ orbitals vacant. Hybridization is $d^2sp^3$ (Inner orbital complex). Statement A is correct.

B. $[MnCl_6]^{3-}$:
$Mn^{3+}$ is $3d^4$. $Cl^-$ is a weak field ligand (WFL). No pairing occurs. To accommodate 6 ligands, it uses $4s$, $4p$, and $4d$ orbitals. Hybridization is $sp^3d^2$ (Outer orbital complex). Statement B is correct.

C. $[CoF_6]^{3-}$:
$Co^{3+}$ is $3d^6$. $F^-$ is a WFL. No pairing occurs. Hybridization is $sp^3d^2$ (Outer orbital complex). Statement C is incorrect because it mentions $d^2sp^3$.

D. $[FeF_6]^{3-}$:
$Fe^{3+}$ is $3d^5$. $F^-$ is a WFL. No pairing occurs. Hybridization is $sp^3d^2$ (Outer orbital complex). Statement D is correct.

E. $[Ni(CN)_4]^{2-}$:
$Ni^{2+}$ is $3d^8$. $CN^-$ is a SFL, causing pairing of the two unpaired electrons in $3d$. This leaves one $3d$ orbital vacant. Hybridization is $dsp^2$ (Square planar, Inner orbital complex). Statement E is incorrect because it mentions $sp^3$.

Correct statements are A, B, and D.

solution:
AgNH32Ag(CN)2→ AgNH32+ +  Ag(CN)2-                                               ( Ag+1)                   ( Ag+1)

Sum of oxidation states of two silver ions = 1 + 1 = 2

q: In which of the following octahedral complex species the magnitude of $\Delta_0$ will be maximum?
question: In which of the following octahedral complex species the magnitude of $\Delta_0$ will be maximum?
solution:
Crystal field splitting depends upon the nature of ligand. The nature of ligand $\Delta$ decreases as shown below
$\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{--} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} < \mathrm{NH}_3 < \mathrm{CN}^{-}$
hence the crystal field splitting will be maximum for $\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}$

q: The metal ion whose electronic configuration is not affected by the nature of the ligand and which gives a violet colour in non-luminous flame under hot condition in borax bead test is
question: The metal ion whose electronic configuration is not affected by the nature of the ligand and which gives a violet colour in non-luminous flame under hot condition in borax bead test is
solution:
$\mathrm{Ni}^{2+}$ gives violet colour with borax bead test in nonluminous flame under hot conditions. $\mathrm{Ni}^{2+}$ has $\mathrm{d}^8$ configuration which does not depend on nature of ligand present in octahedral field.
$\mathrm{Ni}^{2+} \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}_{2 g}^6 \mathrm{eg}^2$

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following complex is homoleptic?
question: Which of the following complex is homoleptic?
solution:
Homoleptic complexes are compounds in which all the ligands bound to the central metal atom or ion are identical.

Homoleptic complexes have only one type of ligand.

Ni(CN)42- has only one type of ligand.

Hence, option A is the answer.

question: The type of hybridization and the magnetic property of $\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_6\right]^{3-}$ are :
options: $d^2 s p^3$, paramagnetic with four unpaired electrons
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\mathrm{MnCl}_6\right]^{3-} \text { contains } \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}} \\ & \mathrm{Mn}^{+3}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4 \\ & \text { Ligand } \Rightarrow \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{WFL})\end{aligned}$

$\text { Hybridisation }=\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$
4 unpaired electrons

question: The oxidation states of iron atoms in compounds <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mfenced></math>, respectively, are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></math>. Then sum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></math> is ........... <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mrow><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>CN</mi></mfenced><mn>5</mn></msub><mfenced><mi>NOS</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></munder><mtext> </mtext><munder><mrow><msub><mi>Na</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mfenced open="[" close="]"><msub><mi>FeO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced></mrow><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mfenced></munder><mtext> </mtext><munder><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><msub><mi>Fe</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><mi>CO</mi></mfenced><mn>9</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mfenced></munder></math>
solution:
Oxidation state of Fe in Na4FeCN5NOS:

Charge of CN =-1

Charge of NOS=-1

Suppose oxidation state of Fe = x

4+x-5-1=0⇒x=+2

Oxidation state of Fe in Na4FeO4:

4+x-8=0⇒x=+4

Oxidation state of Fe in Fe2CO9 =0 because carbonyl is neutral ligand.

The oxidation states of iron in these compounds will be

A = +2   B = +4   C = 0 

The sum of oxidation states will be = 6.

subject: Chemistry
q:
If the CFSE of TiH2O63+ is -96.0 kJ/mol, this complex will absorb maximum at wavelength nm. (nearest integer)

Assume Planck' constant (h)=6.4×10-34Js, Speed of light (c)=3.0×108 m/s and Avogadro's constant NA=6×1023/mol.
question:
If the CFSE of TiH2O63+ is -96.0 kJ/mol, this complex will absorb maximum at wavelength nm. (nearest integer)

Assume Planck' constant (h)=6.4×10-34Js, Speed of light (c)=3.0×108 m/s and Avogadro's constant NA=6×1023/mol.
solution:
In TiH2O63+, oxidation state of Titanium is +3.
Thus, electronic configuration:

Ti+3:3d1⇒t2g1,0,0,eg0,0

We know CFSE =-0.4nt2g+0.6negΔo       (      Δo = Octahedral splitting energy)

Δo=96×1030.4×6×1023=4×10-19 J

Using planck's equation and fact that octahedral splitting energy per complex is:
⇒Δo=hcλ=4×10-19 J

⇒λ=6.4×10-34×3×1084×10-19=4.8×10-7 m

⇒λ=480×10-9 m

∴λ=480 nm

subject: Chemistry
q:
The correct statements from the following are:

A. The strength of anionic ligands can be explained by crystal field theory.

B. Valence bond theory does not give a quantitative interpretation of kinetic stability of coordination compounds.

C. The hybridization involved in formation of [Ni(CN)4] 2– complex is dsp2.

D. The number of possible isomer(s) of cis-[PtCl2 (en)2] 2+ is one

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
The correct statements from the following are:

A. The strength of anionic ligands can be explained by crystal field theory.

B. Valence bond theory does not give a quantitative interpretation of kinetic stability of coordination compounds.

C. The hybridization involved in formation of [Ni(CN)4] 2– complex is dsp2.

D. The number of possible isomer(s) of cis-[PtCl2 (en)2] 2+ is one

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
The strength of ligands discussed in spectrochemical series.

According to the limitations of VBT,

It does not give a quantitative interpretation of the thermodynamic or kinetic stabilities of coordination compounds.

 Hybridisation of [Ni(CN)4] -2 is dsp2.

The outer electronic configuration of Ni2+ ion is 3d84s0.

It is a square planar complex. Cyanide ion is a strong field ligand, thus all electrons will be paired.

One 3d, one 4s and two 4p orbitals undergo dsp2 hybridization, four equivalent dsp2 hybrid orbitals which are occupied by 4 electron pairs of 4 cyanide ligands.

This forms [Ni(CN)4] -2

cis-[PtCl2 (en)2] 2+ complex exhibits optical isomerism, hence, it has two isomers.

Hence statement B and C are correct.

Complex		 LIST-II
Colour

B. K3CoNO26 II. white
C. MnOOH2 III. yellow
D. Fe4FeCN63 IV. blue

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
The formula Mg(NH4)PO4 represents magnesium ammonium phosphate it is white in colour. K3CoNO26 is known as potassium hexanitritocobaltate(III). It is yellow in colour. The yellow colour is due to the presence of the cobalt ion (Co3+) in the complex, which absorbs certain wavelengths of light and reflects yellow light.  MnOOH2is brown in colour. Fe4FeCN63 is blue in colour. 

Complex Colour
MgNH4PO4 White 
K3CoNO26 Yellow
MnOOH2 Brown
Fe4FeCN63 Blue

question: The correct sequence of ligands in the order of decreasing field strength is :
